text.title: UAT Script Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the UAT Script Pattern methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every UAT step traces to ≥1 acceptance-criterion id. Steps without AC reference are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Every UAT step traces to ≥1 acceptance-criterion id. Steps without AC reference are forbidden.

rule.statement: Steps are numbered AND atomic. Step 'login and post a comment' is forbidden; split into 'login', 'post comment'.
rule.rationale: Steps are numbered AND atomic. Step 'login and post a comment' is forbidden; split into 'login', 'post comment'.

rule.statement: Every step has a named expected_result. 'works as expected' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Every step has a named expected_result. 'works as expected' is forbidden.

rule.statement: Record names tester(s) and their email/handle. 'QA' / 'team' / '' forbidden.
rule.rationale: Record names tester(s) and their email/handle. 'QA' / 'team' / '' forbidden.

rule.statement: Sign-off log records timestamp, tester, pass/fail, and any defect ids. Missing → release blocked.
rule.rationale: Sign-off log records timestamp, tester, pass/fail, and any defect ids. Missing → release blocked.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-uat-script-pattern.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
